title: Hugo's School Garden Through the Seasons
synopsis: Hugo discovers the magic of his school garden as it changes through all four seasons, learning about nature's cycle from spring planting to winter rest.

pages.text: Hugo loves his new school! Today, his teacher Mrs. Rose shows the class their special garden. "We'll plant seeds in spring," she says with a smile. Hugo claps his hands with excitement.

illustration.description: A cheerful preschool teacher and children in a school garden with raised beds, budding trees, and soft spring pastel colors. Hugo, a boy with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes, stands among his classmates looking excited and eager to learn.
illustration.alt: Hugo and classmates in spring garden with teacher

pages.text: Hugo carefully places tiny tomato seeds in the dark, rich soil. He pats the earth gently and waters them with a small watering can. "Grow big and strong," Hugo whispers to his seeds.

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes kneeling beside a garden bed, planting seeds with a tiny watering can nearby. Spring flowers bloom around the garden in soft pink, yellow, and green pastels.
illustration.alt: Hugo planting seeds in spring garden bed

pages.text: Summer comes with bright sunshine! Hugo's tomato plants have grown tall with green leaves and tiny yellow flowers. He waters them every day at school. The garden buzzes with happy bees.

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes watering tall tomato plants in bright summer sunlight. The garden is lush and green with vibrant summer colors - bright blues, yellows, and greens. Bees fly around colorful flowers.
illustration.alt: Hugo watering growing tomato plants in summer

pages.text: "Look!" Hugo shouts happily. Small green tomatoes hang from the vines like little green balls. Hugo counts five tiny tomatoes on his plant. He can't wait to see what happens next!

illustration.description: Close-up of Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes pointing excitedly at small green tomatoes growing on the vine. Bright summer colors fill the scene with golden sunshine and vibrant green foliage.
illustration.alt: Hugo discovering green tomatoes on his plant

pages.text: Autumn arrives with orange and red leaves! Hugo's tomatoes have turned bright red and juicy. At snack time, he shares his delicious tomatoes with all his friends. Everyone says "Yummy!"

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes harvesting red tomatoes in an autumn garden. Warm autumn colors of orange, red, and golden yellow surround him as leaves fall gently from trees.
illustration.alt: Hugo harvesting red tomatoes in autumn garden

pages.text: Winter brings snow to the garden. The plants sleep under a white blanket. Hugo looks out the classroom window and smiles. "In spring, we'll plant new seeds," he says. The seasons will come again!

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes looking through a frosty classroom window at the snow-covered garden. Cool winter colors of white, pale blue, and silver create a peaceful scene with bare trees and sleeping garden beds.
illustration.alt: Hugo looking at snowy winter garden through window

metadata.illustrationStyle: seasonal cycle, spring pastels, summer brights, autumn warmth, winter cool, same scene different seasons, time passing, nature calendar, cyclical beauty

questions.question: What did Hugo plant in the spring?
questions.explanation: Hugo planted tomato seeds in the spring garden!

questions.question: What color were Hugo's tomatoes when they were ready to eat?
questions.explanation: The tomatoes turned bright red when they were ripe and ready to eat!

questions.question: What covered the garden in winter?
questions.explanation: Snow covered the garden like a white blanket in winter!

questions.question: Who helped the class with the garden?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Rose was Hugo's teacher who showed them how to garden!
